name: Siva Kalyan Karicheti
headline: I build practical systems where artificial intelligence, enterprise software and data meet.
aboutPrimary: I am an MSc Artificial Intelligence student at ECE Engineering School in Paris, with a B.Tech in Computer Science and Engineering and hands-on ERP development experience.
aboutSecondary: My work spans SAP ABAP, Python, data visualization and web technologies. I enjoy turning technical requirements into reliable solutions and learning the system deeply enough to improve it.

projects.title: NVIDIA logistics workflows
projects.description: Developed Smart Forms for packing lists, commercial invoices and bills of lading; built ALV programs for POD updates; integrated carrier data and updated delivery tracking with BAdI implementations.

projects.title: GuardianAir
projects.description: A sensor-driven airbag inflation concept designed to reduce Sudden Infant Death Syndrome risk through real-time sleep monitoring and responsive IoT safety mechanisms.

projects.title: Student Performance Analytics Dashboard
projects.description:
Modern educational institutions generate vast amounts of student data, spanning
academic performance (grades), attendance logs, and digital engagement
metrics. However, these data streams often remain siloed and underutilized due
to a lack of specialized analytical frameworks. The Student Performance
Analytics Dashboard project successfully addressed this challenge by building an
end-to-end data processing pipeline and an interactive visualization system
tailored for instructors and institutional administrators.

projects.title: Bitcoin and BlockCypher
projects.description: In this project, I gain experience creating transactions using the Bitcoin and BlockCypher testnet blockchains and Bitcoin Script. This project consists of 4 questions, each of which is explained below. The starter code we provide uses python-bitcoinlib, a free, low-level Python~3 library for manipulating Bitcoin transactions. Bitcoin transactions are fundamentally a list of outputs, each of which is associated with an amount of bitcoin that is ``locked'' with a puzzle in the form of a program called a scriptPubKey (also sometimes called a ``smart contract''), and a list of inputs, each of which references an output from the list of outputs and includes the ``answer'' to that output's puzzle in the form of a program called a scriptSig. Validating a scriptSig consists of appending the associated scriptPubKey to it, running the combined script and ensuring that it outputs True.
